feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Analytics Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP Analytics Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAC - Is there any way to change drill limitation in tables by scripting?
entry.content:
SAC has a limitation to show too many rows in a table. The default limit is 500 rows and 60 columns (30000 cells).

Can I set another limit or to set it unlimited by JS scripting? I mean something like "Table_1.[function]()..."

entry.title: SAC onLongPress() event not working on desktop?
entry.content:
Dear Colleagues,

I'm asking for help:

Using Optimized View Story, and for the onLongPress() event of any widget (button, picture) no script runs. Seems like that feature is just not working. Could you please provide some guidance if any additional configuration is needed (e.g. configure duration) to make it work?

many thanks,

Attila

entry.title: Unable to clear, remove any Input Filter after applying it.
entry.content:
Hey Everyone,

How to deselect, clear, reset an Input Filter after applying it on any story/page? 

The only way I am seeing is to delete the entire filter manually from the context menu.

Any suggestions are highly appreciated.  🙂

entry.title: Sap analytics cloud copying bertween versions in model
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I allow users to upload forecast data into a forecast version.

I want to retain the two most recent forecasts so that users can compare the current forecast with the previous one.

I tried using the Data Actions Advanced Editor but haven’t been successful.

How can I achieve this? Any guidance would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: IPS Error when provisioning useHs from IAS to SAC
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I get an IPS error when provisioning users from IAS to SAC.

The first provisioning works fine with no errors, and users are created in SAC.

However, when I provision users to SAC again ( when provisioning user changes) using a READ or RESYNC job, I get the following error:

error=HTTP operation failed invoking https://sactenant/api/v1/scim2/Users/667w-wee3-32ds-23d-23wdad with statusCode: 400, Response: {"schemas":["urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error"],"status":"400","scimType":"invalidValue","detail":"Request is unparsable, syntactically incorrect, or violates schema."},

The Target SAC Transformation Code is:

{
  "user": {
    "condition": "isValidEmail($.emails[0].value) && (('%sac.group.prefix%' === 'null') || ($.groups[?(@.display =~ /%sac.group.prefix%.*/)] empty false))",
    "mappings": [
      {
        "constant": [
          "urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:user-custom-parameters",
          "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User",
          "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User"
        ],
        "targetPath": "$.schemas"
      },
      {
        "sourceVariable": "entityIdTargetSystem",
        "targetPath": "$.id"
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.emails[0].value",
        "targetPath": "$.userName"
      },
      {
        "condition": "$.emails[?(@.primary == true)].value != []",
        "sourcePath": "$.emails[?(@.primary == true)].value",
        "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": false,
        "optional": true,
        "targetPath": "$.userName"
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.userName",
        "optional": true,
        "targetPath": "$.userName"
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.name.givenName",
        "optional": true,
        "targetPath": "$.name.givenName"
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.name.middleName",
        "optional": true,
        "targetPath": "$.name.middleName"
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.name.familyName",
        "optional": true,
        "targetPath": "$.name.familyName"
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.displayName",
        "optional": true,
        "targetPath": "$.displayName"
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.externalId",
        "optional": true,
        "targetPath": "$.externalId"
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.active",
        "optional": true,
        "targetPath": "$.active"
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.emails",
        "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true,
        "targetPath": "$.emails"
      },
      {
        "condition": "$.emails[0].length() > 0",
        "constant": true,
        "targetPath": "$.emails[0].primary"
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.groups[*].value",
        "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true,
        "optional": true,
        "targetPath": "$.groups[?(@.value)]",
        "functions": [
          {
            "entityType": "group",
            "type": "resolveEntityIds"
          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:user-custom-parameters']",
        "optional": true,
        "targetPath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:user-custom-parameters']"
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$.emails[0].value",
        "optional": true,
        "targetPath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:user-custom-parameters']['idpUserId']"
      },
      {
        "condition": "$.emails[?(@.primary == true)].value != []",
        "sourcePath": "$.emails[?(@.primary == true)].value",
        "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": false,
        "optional": true,
        "targetPath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:user-custom-parameters']['idpUserId']"
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:user-custom-parameters']['idpUserId']",
        "optional": true,
        "targetPath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:user-custom-parameters']['idpUserId']"
      },
      {
        "sourcePath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User']['manager']['value']",
        "optional": true,
        "targetPath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User']['manager']['value']",
        "functions": [
          {
            "type": "resolveEntityIds"
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  },

    "group": {
        "condition": "isAttributeWithOptionalPrefix($.displayName, sac.group.prefix) && isAttributeWithOptionalPrefix($['urn:sap:cloud:scim:schemas:extension:custom:2.0:Group']['name'], sac.group.prefix) && (isRegularGroup() || isApplicationSpecificGroup())",
        "mappings": [
            {
                "constant": [
                    "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:Group",
                    "urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:group-roles",
                    "urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:group-custom-parameters"
                ],
                "targetPath": "$.schemas"
            },
            {
                "sourceVariable": "entityIdTargetSystem",
                "targetPath": "$.id"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.displayName",
                "targetPath": "$.displayName",
                "functions": [
                    {
                        "condition": "isAttributeWithMandatoryPrefix(@, sac.group.prefix)",
                        "function": "replaceFirstString",
                        "regex": "%sac.group.prefix%",
                        "replacement": ""
                    }
                ]
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.externalId",
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$.externalId"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.roles",
                "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true,
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$.roles"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$.members[*].value",
                "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true,
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$.members[?(@.value)]",
                "functions": [
                    {
                        "entityType": "user",
                        "type": "resolveEntityIds"
                    }
                ]
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:group-roles']",
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:group-roles']"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:group-custom-parameters']",
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:group-custom-parameters']"
            },
            {
                "sourcePath": "$['urn:sap:cloud:scim:schemas:extension:custom:2.0:Group']['description']",
                "optional": true,
                "targetPath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:group-custom-parameters']['description']"
            }
        ]
    }
}

In SAC, under "Choose a user attribute to map to your provider", "USER ID" is selected.
Dynamic User Creation is disabled.
The USER ID is provisioned from Azure as employeeId.

Any ideas on how to fix this?

Thank you very much.

Best Regards

entry.title: How to disable Export option in SAC Data Analyzer based on SAP BEx query
entry.content:
Hi,

How to disable Export option in SAC Data Analyzer based on SAP BEx query

entry.title: want to make a comparison chart having two product like A and B, and show the match and non match %
entry.content:
I want to make a comparison chart having two product like A and B, to show the matching % and not matching % and also see the result for match and not match value in table when we click on match % and not match %?
I'm getting the % for match and not match but when I'm adding a dimension for filter, the % value are not coming in chart for product A & B, it's coming for all the records present in dimension.

  1. We need %
  2. We need filter

Please let me know how can we achieve this.

entry.title: Can Rapid FP&A be used with S/4HANA 2022 On-Premise, and how to configure Standard CDS?
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am currently working on implementing Rapid FP&A in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) with S/4HANA 2022 On-Premise. The documentation I’ve come across primarily mentions SAP S/4HANA Public & Private Cloud Editions, but I haven’t found clear guidance for S/4HANA On-Premise.

My Key Questions:
Can we use the Rapid FP&A Suite with the 2022 On-Premise version of S/4HANA?
The documentation and resources mention connectivity primarily for cloud editions, and I would like confirmation on whether the suite is compatible with on-premise.
Steps to Configure Standard CDS Views for On-Premise:
I am trying to configure the Standard CDS (for example, GLAccountBalanceFlowQuery found in the SAP_FI_FPA_IM_S4FinancialPlan model) in our on-premise S/4HANA environment.
We are facing issues, such as permissions errors and inability to refresh queries, which seem to be cloud-related setups. However, we are on S/4HANA On-Premise 2022.

I've looked for existing Standard CDS views related to FP&A in our on-premise system, but I haven't found relevant resources specific to on-premise.
Encountered a permission issue with GLAccountBalanceFlowQuery during refresh attempts (attached error).


Can the community help clarify whether the Rapid FP&A suite can be used with S/4HANA On-Premise?
If so, could you guide me through the steps required to configure the relevant Standard CDS views (or build custom ones) for FP&A models on-premise?
Any detailed steps, documentation, or guidance would be highly appreciated!

Thank you in advance for your help.

entry.title: Expense Waterfall is upside down
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

I noticed that waterfalls which only display only expenses (account dimension is used of course) are upside down. The rest of alternative widgets (bars, lines, numeric points) are correctly displayed, but not the waterfalls?! It appears to me very counterintuitive to read such a widget, especially when trying to explain variances between two periods. 

Any thoughts about it? Anyone in finance already faced this challenge?

Thank you

entry.title: Aggregation on Data Action
entry.content:
Hello all, 

Can anyone help me resolve the issue I'm facing? I need to calculate the value of Revenue NA [DRV0060] by multiplying the lookup price and units, but the resulting values are incorrect. I'm implementing this using scripting in a data action, and the values appearing are significantly lower than the expected ones, which are simply obtained by multiplying the two factors. I suspect the issue is due to improper aggregation at the hierarchical level. 

Script I am using 
DATA([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0060") =
RESULTLOOKUP([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0055") * RESULTLOOKUP([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0020")

below are the incorrect values

entry.title: SAC - retrieving stories connected to specific model
entry.content:
Hello, 

We have a lot of stories in our tenant, and sometimes we need to know which stories are connected to which model. Model are live connection to BW4HANA. 
Is there some dedicated tool for it ? Or what is best approach to do it ?
Thanks for answers
Viktor

entry.title: SAC Story Drill-Through Not Working Across Different Models
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am experiencing an issue with drill-through functionality in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). When I use a hyperlink in an SAC story to drill through to another story with the same model, everything works as expected, and I am directed to the specific ID I selected.

However, when I try to drill through to a story that uses a different model (even though I copied the model and the data is identical), I am not taken to the specific ID. Instead, the entire story is displayed without filtering down to the ID.

I have verified that the data and setup are the same, but the only difference is the model. Does anyone have any suggestions or solutions for making the drill-through work when using different models?

Any help would be appreciated!

Thank you in advance.


Same Model:




Different Models:

entry.title: Access detail story from main story
entry.content:
Hi team,

we have created main (parent) story and detail (child) story. Detail story is setup to be opened from main story through External URL option. Both main and detail stories are in same folder. Now my requirement is user should not be able to open detail story independently. He should be able to open it from parent story only. Can this be achieved?

entry.title: How to Create " Insert Sections" in SAP SAC Tables (Similar to Webi?)
entry.content:
In SAP Web Intelligence (Webi), the "Insert Section" option allows us to split a table into separate sections based on a selected dimension. However, I can't find a direct equivalent of this feature in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC).

Scenario:

  • My dataset includes Business Unit and Employees as dimensions, along with Few measures.
  • The number of Business Units and Employees changes dynamically.
  • In Webi, I could use "insert section" option on Business Unit, which would automatically create separate tables for each Business Unit available, displaying its employees and measures.
  • In SAC, I need a way to achieve the same result—showing all Business Units simultaneously in separate sections—without using filters, as the business requirement is to see all Business units at once.

My Requirement is ; How can I replicate Or Achieve Insert Section functionality in SAC to dynamically display separate tables for each Business Unit?

entry.title: SAP Sales Cloud V2: Reporting Capabilities
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I'm currently exploring the capabilities of SAP Sales Cloud Version 2, particularly regarding reporting.

I'd love to hear your experiences and opinions on:

  1. What key improvements does SAP Sales Cloud V2 offer compared to previous versions in terms of reporting?

  2. What are the main tools or functionalities available for creating advanced reports?

  3. Is there sufficient flexibility to customize reports based on specific business requirements?

  4. Have you integrated SAP Sales Cloud V2 with external analytics tools? How has your experience been?

  5. Is reporting always based on SAP Analytics Cloud, or does SAP Sales Cloud V2 have its own built-in reporting tool?

  6. Are there any predefined reports available that work immediately once connected?

I greatly appreciate any information or advice you can share.

Thank you very much!

entry.title: SAP Datasphere and SAP Information Steward Connectivity
entry.content:
Hi,

We are currently migrating to SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud. We have currently BO, Data Services and Information Steward solutions deployed.

We wanted to know how we can integrate SAP Information Steward with SAP Datasphere?.  What are the ways to connect IS to Datasphere?

Regards,

Nachiket

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Planning : Advance Formulas (Aggregation)
entry.content:
Hey all, 

can anyone of you help me need to calculate the product of aggregated account dimensions (Units and Lookup Price) across the Product and Time dimensions. However, when I directly multiply these accounts using Data Actions, the calculation is performed row-wise first and then aggregated, leading to incorrect results. Instead, I want to first aggregate the values by Product and Time and then perform the multiplication to ensure accuracy. Can anyone help me with how to achieve this using scripting? I have to claculate the Revenue NA for NorthEast Region 

please help this is the code that I wrote  just to check th value for units only but no result is appearing 

MEMBERSET [d/ENTITY] = "REG0001"
VARIABLEMEMBER #SUMOFUNITS OF [d/ACCOUNT]
DATA([d/ACCOUNT]=#SUMOFUNITS, [d/PRODUCT]="#") = RESULTLOOKUP ([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0020" )
DATA([d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0060" ) = RESULTLOOKUP([d/ACCOUNT] = #SUMOFUNITS , [d/PRODUCT]= "#")

alternative workaround but still values are not appearing 

MEMBERSET [d/ENTITY] = "REG0001"
MEMBERSET [d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0020"
AGGREGATE_DIMENSIONS = [d/PRODUCT],[d/ACCOUNT]
AGGREGATE_WRITETO [d/ACCOUNT] = "DRV0060"
AGGREGATE_WRITETO [d/PRODUCT] = "#"

entry.title: Return blank value using IF () in story report
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have a requirement to populate a field value as below in story reports:

If Field Value matches to particular value, then Return "Last Modified Date Time" else Return Blank.

I tried to achieve it using IF () Function, but system is expecting second parameter of IF () Function also as Date Time type value, not blank. 

Any assistance would be really helpful.

entry.title: Join in DataSphere
entry.content:
Hi team, 

I have two excel files that have This columns:

Excel1:

Excel 2:

I want to join this data into a table to display it in SAC, but when I perform the join, no data appears.

 

 

The data itself is similar but also different at the same time, as it serves different purposes.

Thanks,

Ganjos

entry.title: SAC: Bug ? 2 Live Data Models: After Changeing the Query Inputvariable the SAC Screen pops up Twice
entry.content:
Hi,

thats a bug ?

After changeing a live data models query variable (BW),
resaveing the sac query model,
deleting and linking the SAC query variables again,
I still get two SAC variable input screens .

The layout of the SAC story is too complex to build the story from scratch again,

Any solution ?

In Detail:

A)  Got 2 Live data models = 2 Queries with nearly the same parameters:

 

B) Linked the 2 live data models, queries, in the SAC Story:

C) Running the SAC Story I falsely get  the input variable screen twice.

D) Building a SAC prototyp with the two models and linking them as above works fine.

The story is too complex to build it from scratch again.

Any other solution available ?
Is this a bug ?

A bug fiy possible ?

ThanXs

Martin
